results.description:
AI / Machine Learning Engineer

San Diego, CA
In-office | Full-Time

About the Opportunity

Our client, a fast-growing AI solutions and advisory firm, is seeking an AI / Machine Learning Engineer to help design, build, and scale advanced AI systems powering real-world business applications.

This role is ideal for an engineer who combines deep machine learning expertise with hands-on experience deploying LLM–based solutions into production environments. You will play a key role in architecting intelligent systems that move beyond experimentation and deliver measurable operational value.

Role Summary

As a core member of the engineering team, you will design and implement AI-driven applications including retrieval-augmented systems, conversational agents, document intelligence solutions, and generative content tools. You will collaborate closely with product, data, and strategy teams to translate business objectives into scalable AI architectures.

The ideal candidate has strong experience working with leading commercial and open-source LLM ecosystems and understands how to optimize, evaluate, and productionize AI systems responsibly and efficiently.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and deploy machine learning and LLM-based solutions across use cases such as document processing (OCR), RAG pipelines, AI agents, and generative applications.
  • Develop, test, and refine prompt strategies to improve output quality and alignment with business requirements.
  • Evaluate, fine-tune, and benchmark ML and language models using structured experimentation and performance metrics.
  • Build and maintain data pipelines for model training, evaluation, and deployment workflows.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to integrate AI capabilities into client-facing platforms and internal tools.
  • Deploy models to production environments and monitor system performance, reliability, and cost efficiency.
  • Stay current with advancements in generative AI, deep learning, and emerging frameworks to continuously enhance system capabilities.

Required Qualifications

  • 5+ years of experience in AI/ML engineering or related roles.

  • Bachelors or Masters degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical discipline.
  • Hands-on experience working with major LLM platforms (commercial APIs and open-source models).
  • Strong background building retrieval-augmented generation systems, LLM pipelines, or agent-based architectures.
  • Proficiency in Python and experience with deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch (or equivalent).
  • Solid foundation in machine learning, neural networks, and natural language processing.
  • Experience evaluating model performance using appropriate validation and benchmarking techniques.
  • Experience working with relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL) and modern data warehouse systems.

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Ability to communicate effectively across technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Preferred Experience

  • Contributions to open-source AI projects or involvement in research communities.

  • Experience deploying AI systems on major cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure).
  • Familiarity with distributed data processing technologies (e.g., Spark or similar frameworks).
  • Exposure to domain applications in sectors such as financial services, healthcare, retail, or enterprise technology.

Why Consider This Opportunity?

  • Work on production-grade AI systems with real business impact.

  • Join a technically strong and execution-focused team.

  • Contribute to innovative applications of generative AI across industries.

  • Be part of a company scaling its applied AI capabilities nationally.

AI Solutions Strategist

San Diego, CA
On-site | Full-Time

About the Opportunity

Our client, an innovative and rapidly growing AI-focused consulting firm, is seeking an AI Solutions Strategist to join their San Diego-based team. This organization partners with businesses to design and implement practical artificial intelligence solutions that streamline operations, modernize workflows, and drive measurable outcomes.

This is an opportunity to work at the intersection of strategy, technology, and client delivery in a highly collaborative environment.

Position Overview

The AI Solutions Strategist will lead client engagements from discovery through implementation and adoption. This individual will work directly with stakeholders to identify high-impact AI use cases, define solution strategies, collaborate with technical teams, and ensure successful delivery against project commitments.

The ideal candidate combines strategic thinking, strong project execution skills, and hands-on familiarity with AI-driven solutions.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead structured discovery sessions to identify business needs and define prioritized AI initiatives within project scope.
  • Translate client objectives into clearly defined solution plans and deliverables prior to execution.
  • Collaborate closely with engineering teams during prototyping and implementation phases.
  • Analyze operational workflows to uncover automation and AI optimization opportunities.
  • Design and recommend AI-enabled process improvements.
  • Manage project timelines and ensure delivery meets quality standards and client expectations.
  • Support client training and adoption to ensure long-term success of implemented solutions.
  • Track progress, utilization, and deliverables within project management systems.
  • Gather feedback and iterate on solutions to continuously improve outcomes.

What Success Looks Like

  • Consistent, on-time delivery of defined project milestones.

  • High-quality AI solutions that generate tangible business impact.

  • Strong collaboration across strategy, technical, and client stakeholders.

  • Clear communication and structured execution throughout engagements.

Required Qualifications

  • Experience in AI strategy, AI implementation, LLM-based solutions, or related technology consulting.
  • Demonstrated project management and client delivery experience, preferably in technology or consulting environments.
  • Strong ability to analyze workflows and identify optimization opportunities﻿
  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate technical concepts into business value.
  • Highly organized, self-motivated, and comfortable managing multiple client engagements.

  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Direct client-facing experience in AI or automation initiatives from discovery through delivery.
  • Familiarity with modern project management tools (e.g., ClickUp, Asana, Jira, or similar platforms).
  • Background in consulting, workflow automation, or digital transformation.
  • Working knowledge of AI/ML systems, LLM tools, or rapid prototyping environments.

Compensation

Base salary range: $65,000 – $90,000 annually, doe.

Business Development Associate | Luxury Travel Contractor | Remote

We are recruiting a Business Development Associate on behalf of a high-profile, boutique luxury travel company based in Los Angeles. Built on an exceptional reputation and an elite client roster, this company has grown almost entirely through word of mouth — and now they are ready to grow intentionally. This is a rare opportunity to sell something people genuinely love buying.

The Opportunity

You will identify and win new business within the mid-market entertainment sector — think production companies, talent agencies, management firms, and entertainment-adjacent businesses in the 25-100 employee range. Once you close, the internal team takes over all account management. Your job is to open doors and bring clients in.

What You'll Do

  • Prospect and generate your own leads within the entertainment industry
  • Build relationships with decision-makers at mid-size entertainment companies booking corporate and talent travel
  • Pitch and close new accounts for luxury travel services
  • Collaborate with the internal team on handoff once business is won

What You Bring

  • A natural networker with an existing footprint in entertainment, media, or adjacent industries
  • Proven track record of hunting and closing new business — not just managing existing accounts
  • Self-directed and comfortable working independently without a structured pipeline handed to you
  • Polished, credible, and at ease in high-end client environments
  • Understanding of the luxury market and the expectations that come with it

Preferred Qualifications

  • Background in luxury travel, hospitality, or entertainment services
  • Existing relationships with mid-size entertainment companies or talent-adjacent businesses
  • Experience as an independent sales contractor or consultant

Compensation

Uncapped commission | Compensation structure discussed with qualified candidates

Business Development Lead | AI Consulting San Diego, CA · Full-time, In-Office

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Business Development Lead to join a fast-growing AI consulting firm. This is a high-visibility role for a proven sales professional with a track record of building a pipeline independently and closing complex, consultative engagements at the executive level.

Reporting directly to the Founder, the Business Development Lead will own the firm's full commercial motion — from initial prospecting through contract execution — while simultaneously establishing the sales infrastructure and processes that will support long-term revenue growth.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage the full sales cycle from outbound prospecting through signed contract and client onboarding handoff
  • Build and maintain a qualified pipeline through outbound outreach, networking, referrals, and strategic partnerships
  • Lead consultative discovery and solution positioning with executive and C-suite stakeholders
  • Scope and present tailored AI consulting proposals in collaboration with technical delivery teams
  • Develop repeatable pipeline processes, reporting frameworks, and sales discipline to support scale
  • Partner directly with the Founder on go-to-market strategy, key accounts, and firm positioning

Qualifications

  • 3–6+ years of B2B sales or business development experience in consulting, professional services, or technical solutions
  • Demonstrated ability to independently own and close complex, solution-oriented deals
  • Established executive presence with experience navigating C-suite buying processes
  • Highly self-directed with the ability to build processes and execute simultaneously
  • Comfortable operating in an entrepreneurial environment with limited infrastructure

Compensation

  • Competitive Base salary + Commission

Sales Associate, Reservations

Full-Time/Onsite Orlando, Florida

ABOUT THE ROLE

A well-established travel brand is expanding its sales team. As a Reservations Sales Advisor, you will handle inbound calls from customers who are ready to explore their options for premium cruise and land vacation packages. Your job is to listen carefully, ask the right questions, and guide them toward a booking they will be excited about.

Travel industry experience is not required. Full training is provided. What cannot be trained is genuine curiosity about people and a competitive desire to close. If those come naturally to you, the rest is learnable.

DAY TO DAY

  • Take inbound sales calls and build real rapport with prospective customers from the first hello
  • Understand each customer's wishlist and match them with the right vacation package
  • Provide accurate, timely quotes and walk customers through their options confidently
  • Pivot gracefully when a first suggestion does not fit and find a solution that does
  • Hit performance targets across conversion rate, average margin, data capture, and call handling
  • Complete booking administration accurately and without errors
  • Contribute positively to a team environment that moves fast and values collaboration
  • Follow FTC guidelines, applicable state Seller of Travel regulations, and data protection requirements

SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE

  • Prior experience in a sales role; a call center background is helpful but not required
  • A competitive, self-motivated attitude with a track record of hitting goals
  • Strong listening and interpersonal skills that put customers at ease
  • Organized and accurate under pressure with great attention to detail
  • Flexibility to work evenings and weekends on a rotating basis
  • Comfortable with standard business software including Excel, Word, and Outlook
  • Second language ability is a welcome bonus

BENEFITS

  • Competitive base salary plus commission
  • Health insurance
  • 401(k) with company contribution
  • FAM trip participation: travel on the packages you sell and experience them firsthand

Director of AI and Automation

Location: San Diego, CA (In-Office) | Type: Full-Time

Role Overview

We are recruiting on behalf of a growing AI consulting and implementation firm based in San Diego. This is a foundational leadership hire reporting directly to the President. The Director of AI and Automation will own the internal AI strategy, data infrastructure, and team build from scratch, creating the systems and capabilities that underpin how the business delivers results for its clients.

This is a player-coach role. The right candidate sets strategy and owns architecture decisions while staying close enough to the work to design the right solutions, catch problems early, and execute when needed. Someone who can only manage and someone who can only build are both the wrong fit.

Key Responsibilities

AI Strategy, Infrastructure and Deployment

  • Own the AI and data strategy across the business, covering governance, architecture, accessibility, and deployment
  • Assess and remediate current data infrastructure, including CRM data, inventory systems, and financial reporting
  • Architect and oversee cloud-native data warehouse solutions with a focus on scalability, cost efficiency, and cross-location accessibility (Snowflake strongly preferred; BigQuery or Redshift also valued)
  • Design and oversee modern ELT/ETL pipelines using dbt, Airflow, Fivetran, and Python-based frameworks
  • Define and enforce data governance standards including ownership, quality controls, lineage tracking, and access management
  • Lead customer-facing AI implementation across CRM, sales, and service including lead scoring, follow-up automation, and AI-assisted workflows
  • Evaluate existing AI tools for proper implementation and redundancy; many tools are already in place and need to be made to actually work
  • Identify and execute AI and automation initiatives with clear ROI tied to operational efficiency, customer retention, and revenue performance
  • Evaluate and manage vendor and technology partner relationships across the AI and data stack

Stakeholder Leadership

  • Serve as the primary AI advisor to the executive team, translating technical strategy into clear business decisions
  • Partner with department leads to understand real operational pain points and design solutions that work in practice
  • Run executive-level reporting and strategy sessions with clarity and confidence
  • Drive change management and adoption; accountable for whether people actually use what gets built, not just whether it was built

Team Building

  • Build and lead a lean, high-performing AI team; execute without needing to build a large department first
  • Define what to build internally, what to hire for in Latin America, and where to rely on external partners
  • Work alongside the existing BI team to strengthen capability and establish a clear path for the data organization
  • Manage team performance, provide coaching and mentorship, and build a culture of accountability

Requirements

Essential

  • 8 to 12 or more years in data engineering, data architecture, or a closely related technical role, with at least 3 to 5 years in senior leadership with cross-functional accountability
  • Strong hands-on data infrastructure and cleanup experience; you have fixed broken data environments and established governance where data quality was a real problem, not just a future aspiration
  • Hands-on experience with modern data stack: Snowflake (strongly preferred), dbt, Fivetran, Airflow, BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, or Looker), and cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
  • Deep proficiency in SQL and Python with production pipeline and transformation experience
  • Practical experience deploying AI and ML solutions in production; real systems used by real people, not proofs of concept
  • Retail, CRM, or customer-facing implementation experience in consumer-facing businesses where lead management and customer lifecycle tools are central to operations
  • Equally comfortable architecting a data warehouse and presenting a strategy roadmap to executive leadership
  • Experience hiring and managing technical teams including distributed or offshore resources
  • Comfortable creating structure where none exists and moving without a perfectly defined mandate

Desirable

  • Spanish language proficiency; this role includes building and leading engineering and analytics resources in Latin America and candidates with this experience will receive priority consideration
  • Experience with Snowflake Cortex, Snowpark, Databricks, or BigQuery
  • Background as a founder or early-stage data leader who has owned the full problem before building a team

Competitve Compensation

Director of Growth & Brand

San Diego, CA
On-site | Full-Time

About the Opportunity

Our client, a rapidly scaling AI advisory and implementation firm, is seeking a Director of Growth & Brand to build and lead its marketing function from the ground up.

This is a highly strategic and visible leadership role reporting closely to the executive team. The right candidate will shape how the market understands practical AI implementation, elevate the firms national reputation, and drive measurable pipeline growth through a sophisticated, premium brand presence.

The organization serves mid-market and enterprise operators seeking real-world AI integration—not experimentation—and is positioning itself as a trusted partner in that transformation.

What You'll Be Responsible For

Brand Strategy & Market Positioning

  • Define and evolve the company's brand as a premium, results-oriented AI advisory partner.
  • Develop clear messaging frameworks tailored to distinct buyer audiences and industries.
  • Ensure consistent positioning across all channels and client touch points.

Thought Leadership & Content Ecosystem

  • Own and execute a comprehensive content strategy across LinkedIn, newsletters, video, long-form insights, and executive commentary.
  • Translate internal frameworks, case studies, and leadership insights into compelling external narratives.
  • Position senior leadership as authoritative voices in AI-driven transformation.
  • Manage creative collaborators including designers, editors, and video partners.

Public Relations & External Visibility

  • Develop and manage media strategy, including earned placements and speaking opportunities.
  • Build relationships that increase visibility among executive and investor audiences.

Demand Generation & Inbound Pipeline

  • Architect full-funnel marketing systems that convert awareness into qualified opportunities.
  • Create high-value lead assets such as executive guides, frameworks, and playbooks.
  • Oversee email campaigns, nurture sequences, and SEO strategy.

  • Track and optimize inbound contribution to revenue pipeline.

Events & Executive Experiences

  • Lead strategy and execution for webinars, executive roundtables, and curated industry events.
  • Oversee promotion, creative direction, and post-event ROI analysis.
  • Ensure events produce tangible business impact, not just attendance metrics.

Community Brand Leadership

  • Drive sponsorship engagement and partner relationships.

  • Oversee marketing and growth strategy for the firms affiliated executive AI community.
  • Expand community visibility and value to members while supporting overall brand authority.

Website & Digital Presence

  • Maintain landing pages, case studies, and lead capture systems.

  • Continuously improve digital performance metrics.

  • Own strategy, messaging, UX, and conversion optimization across digital properties.

Sales Enablement & GTM Assets

  • Develop and refine all client-facing collateral including capability decks, industry briefs, case studies, and service overviews.
  • Partner closely with sales and strategy teams to ensure alignment between messaging and revenue goals.

Executive Brand Development

  • Lead ghostwritten thought leadership and content frameworks.

  • Convert executive insights into scalable top-of-funnel influence.

  • Support senior leadership in developing individual executive visibility.

Marketing Analytics & Performance

  • Own reporting across lead flow, funnel metrics, campaign performance, engagement analytics, and pipeline contribution.
  • Build dashboards and leverage data to refine strategy and drive continuous improvement.

Ideal Background

  • 7+ years of experience in B2B marketing, growth strategy, or brand leadership (professional services, consulting, or high-growth technology environments preferred).
  • Demonstrated ability to elevate or scale a brand to a leadership position within its market.
  • Strong strategic orientation combined with hands-on execution capability.
  • Exceptional written communication skills suited for executive and operator audiences.
  • Experience with modern marketing platforms (e.g., CRM automation tools, CMS platforms, LinkedIn Ads, analytics tools, SEO software).
  • Proven ability to manage external agencies, contractors, and creative partners.
  • Comfortable operating in fast-moving, high-growth environments with evolving priorities.
  • Familiarity with AI-enabled productivity and content tools is strongly preferred.

Compensation

Base salary range: $80,000 – $115,000 annually, doe.

Senior Staff Accountant

We are partnering with a growing medical practice in Southern California to find a talented Senior Staff Accountant to join their finance team. This is a great opportunity for an experienced accounting professional who wants real ownership of the close process, meaningful visibility into the business, and room to grow within a well-established organization.

Location: Hybrid — Temecula, CA (remote-first with occasional on-site visits)

Commutable from Riverside County, Orange County, and North San Diego County.

Type: Full-time

Reports to: Controller

The Opportunity

You'll work closely with the Controller to manage day-to-day accounting operations, support the full close cycle, and deliver accurate financial reporting that leadership relies on. This isn't a back-office-only role — you'll collaborate cross-functionally, help improve processes, and have a genuine impact on how the finance function runs.

What You'll Do

  • Own assigned portions of the monthly, quarterly, and annual close, including journal entries, reconciliations, accruals, variance analysis, and supporting schedules
  • Prepare, analyze, and review financial statements including balance sheets, P&L, cash flow statements, and internal management reports
  • Reconcile subsidiary ledgers (A/R, A/P, payroll, fixed assets) to the general ledger
  • Monitor accounting data to identify trends, variances, and opportunities for cost savings or revenue improvement
  • Support budgeting, forecasting, and financial modeling in collaboration with department leaders
  • Perform intercompany reconciliations across multiple entities and locations
  • Ensure compliance with GAAP, internal controls, and audit requirements
  • Support external auditors during annual financial statement and compliance audits
  • Identify opportunities to streamline workflows, improve controls, and enhance reporting efficiency
  • Provide guidance and mentorship to junior accounting staff as needed

What We're Looking For

  • Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field (required)
  • CPA license or eligibility strongly preferred
  • 4–7 years of progressive accounting experience; healthcare industry background a plus
  • Strong working knowledge of GAAP and general ledger accounting
  • Experience with accounting systems such as QuickBooks, NetSuite, or Sage Intacct
  • Advanced Excel skills including pivot tables, lookup functions, and financial data analysis
  • Proven ability to work independently and stay organized in a hybrid or remote environment
  • Strong analytical mindset, sharp attention to detail, and clear communication skills
  • High level of integrity and professionalism in handling confidential information

Senior Talent Acquisition Specialist – Tech/AI Recruiting

About the Role We're partnering with a fast-growing SaaS company to hire a Senior Talent Acquisition Specialist. This is a senior, hands-on role focused on leading complex technical searches and shaping recruiting strategy at scale.

What You'll Do

  • Own full-cycle recruiting for senior, critical, and hard-to-fill technical roles
  • Design and execute recruiting programs, frameworks, and workforce planning strategies
  • Act as a strategic advisor to executives and cross-functional leaders
  • Drive market intelligence, sourcing strategy, and data-informed hiring decisions
  • Mentor peers and elevate the overall capability of the TA function

What You Bring

  • 10+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience
  • Deep expertise in hiring in AI, Machine Learning, and Software Engineering
  • Strong stakeholder management and executive influence
  • Fluency with AI-powered recruiting tools, ATS platforms (Workable and LinkedIn Recruiter preferred), and talent analytics
  • Bachelor's degree in a technical or business field﻿

Compensation 

$159K–$196K base + equity (RSUs) + comprehensive benefits package﻿

Sales Associate, Reservations

Full-Time/Onsite Orlando, Florida

ABOUT THE ROLE

A well-established travel brand is expanding its sales team. As a Reservations Sales Advisor, you will handle inbound calls from customers who are ready to explore their options for premium cruise and land vacation packages. Your job is to listen carefully, ask the right questions, and guide them toward a booking they will be excited about.

Travel industry experience is not required. Full training is provided. What cannot be trained is genuine curiosity about people and a competitive desire to close. If those come naturally to you, the rest is learnable.

DAY TO DAY

  • Take inbound sales calls and build real rapport with prospective customers from the first hello
  • Understand each customer's wishlist and match them with the right vacation package
  • Provide accurate, timely quotes and walk customers through their options confidently
  • Pivot gracefully when a first suggestion does not fit and find a solution that does
  • Hit performance targets across conversion rate, average margin, data capture, and call handling
  • Complete booking administration accurately and without errors
  • Contribute positively to a team environment that moves fast and values collaboration
  • Follow FTC guidelines, applicable state Seller of Travel regulations, and data protection requirements

SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE

  • Prior experience in a sales role; a call center background is helpful but not required
  • A competitive, self-motivated attitude with a track record of hitting goals
  • Strong listening and interpersonal skills that put customers at ease
  • Organized and accurate under pressure with great attention to detail
  • Flexibility to work evenings and weekends on a rotating basis
  • Comfortable with standard business software including Excel, Word, and Outlook
  • Second language ability is a welcome bonus

BENEFITS

  • Competitive base salary plus commission
  • Health insurance
  • 401(k) with company contribution
  • FAM trip participation: travel on the packages you sell and experience them firsthand
